pattern: Because the rows concatenate into one sorted sequence, a single binary search over a flattened index locates the target in logarithmic time.
hints: Each row is sorted and every value in a row is below the first value of the next, so reading the matrix row by row gives one fully sorted list.
You do not need to actually flatten it: an index into that virtual list maps to a row and column by dividing and taking the remainder by the column count.
Binary search the range from 0 to rows*cols-1, convert the midpoint index to its cell, and compare with the target to halve the search each step.

edge_inputs.description: target present in a middle row
target is the very first element
target is the very last element

stress.description: random sorted matrix with targets that hit and miss
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 10)
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    total = m * n
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(-10000, 10001), total))
    matrix = [vals[i * n:(i + 1) * n] for i in range(m)]
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        target = rng.choice(vals)
    else:
        target = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
    return [matrix, target]
stress.note: half the targets are guaranteed present, half random
